clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Alcohol intake reduction to prevent or treat elevated BP and hypertension
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial visit, hypertension diagnosis, elevated BP screening, medication adjustment
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommend alcohol reduction goal + drink limits by sex

navigationPath.purpose: Display standard drink definitions and limits
Present recommended reduction goals (abstinence or ≤1/≤2 drinks/d)
Schedule follow-up for alcohol reduction progress

validationRules: If sex == female → max recommended = 1 drink/d
If sex == male → max recommended = 2 drinks/d

rules: Level of Evidence: A (High-quality evidence from >1 RCT or meta-analyses)
Target population: Adults with or without hypertension who currently consume alcohol
Secondary goal: Reduction to ≤1 drink/d (women) or ≤2 drinks/d (men)
Standard drink definition: 12-14g alcohol = 12oz beer (5% ABV), 5oz wine (12% ABV), or 1.5oz distilled spirits (40% ABV)
If current intake > recommended → flag for counseling
If patient unwilling to abstain → recommend reduction strategy

recommendedNextStep.description: e.g., 'Reduce to ≤1 drink/d for women or ≤2 drinks/d for men'

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Counsel on alcohol reduction strategies; refer to addiction medicine if unable to reduce
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Reassess alcohol intake at next visit (3-6 months)
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Monitor BP after alcohol reduction (4-12 weeks)
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients with alcohol use disorder for care team notification
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log current intake, reduction goal, and counseling provided to health record
